subject: Re: Merging datasets with different handedness
body:
Hi Mat,
I think you’ll probably need to rename the tomostar files too but otherwise what you describe sounds good!
Re: handedness I don’t think there is an easy solution… you will have to figure out the transforms between current and flipped tomograms 
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Jan 20, 2025, at 07:27, cla...@gmail.com <clazzy@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿
Hi all,
I am working with a sample which has three datasets at the same pixel size. I have processed two separately to 8A and tried to merge them in Relion 5. It sort of worked (more on that in a moment) but I have a couple of questions about the best way to handle things.
My first question is a question of logistics: both are collected with the name Position_001, Position_002, etc. They will need to be differently named to ensure they don't clash, so can I just rename the XML files to dataset1_Position_001.xml and update _rlnMicrographName in the star file accordingly or is there more that I need to change?
Secondly, when I merged my data, the map looked dreadful. I then realised that the datasets are not in the same hand, which likely results from me having a tilt axis of around -90 deg in one and +90 deg in the other. What would be the best way to go about flipping the hand for one of the datasets? If I change the AxisAngle signs manually in the XML files, will this have repercussions on the particle positions and the defocus handedness?
Cheers,
Mat
